app_summary: RedReader is a Reddit client application that allows users to browse and interact with Reddit content through the Reddit API. Users can log in, view subreddit lists, manage their subscriptions, and customize various settings such as theme, font scale, and network behavior.

core_workflows.description: The user starts at the initial state, where they are prompted to accept the Reddit user agreement. After accepting, the user navigates to the front page, which displays a timestamp and various subreddit options. The user then selects a subreddit, which transitions them to a subreddit-specific view, and subsequently returns to the front page.
core_workflows.purpose: This workflow is crucial for allowing users to access subreddit content and navigate back to the front page.

core_workflows.description: The user starts at the initial state and is prompted to log in or proceed anonymously. Choosing to log in prompts a login page, from which the user can continue to a subreddit list or return to the front page with anonymous access.
core_workflows.purpose: This workflow provides the option for users to log in or proceed without logging in, enhancing user flexibility.

core_workflows.description: The user starts at the initial state, accepts the user agreement, and navigates to the front page. From there, they access the settings menu, adjust font scales, and navigate through various settings categories. Finally, they return to the front page.
core_workflows.purpose: This workflow is essential for users who wish to customize their experience, such as adjusting font scales and navigating through different settings.

core_workflows.description: The user begins at the initial state, accepts the user agreement, and navigates to the front page. They then access the settings menu, navigate back to the front page, and eventually return to the front page again.
core_workflows.purpose: This workflow allows users to quickly access settings and return to subreddit browsing.

core_workflows.description: The user starts at the initial state, accepts the user agreement, and navigates to the front page. They then navigate to a specific subreddit, manage their account, and return to the front page.
core_workflows.purpose: This workflow provides a path for users to manage their accounts and subreddit subscriptions.

core_workflows.description: The user starts at the initial state, accepts the user agreement, and navigates to the front page. They then select a subreddit, navigate back to the front page, manage their account, and return to the front page.
core_workflows.purpose: This workflow enables users to navigate through subreddits and manage account settings efficiently.

core_workflows.description: The user starts at the initial state, accepts the user agreement, and navigates to the front page. They then select a subreddit, navigate back to the front page, manage their account, and return to the front page.
core_workflows.purpose: This workflow allows users to manage account settings and subreddit subscriptions.

core_workflows.description: The user starts at the initial state, accepts the user agreement, and navigates to the front page. They then select a subreddit, navigate back to the front page, manage their account, and return to the front page.
core_workflows.purpose: This workflow enables users to manage account settings and subreddit subscriptions efficiently.

core_workflows.description: The user starts at the initial state, accepts the user agreement, and navigates to the front page. They then select a subreddit, navigate back to the front page, manage their account, and repeat the account management process before returning to the front page.
core_workflows.purpose: This workflow demonstrates the ability to manage account settings multiple times.

core_workflows.description: The user starts at the initial state, accepts the user agreement, and navigates to the front page. They then select a subreddit, navigate back to the front page, manage their account, return to the front page, and repeat the account management process before returning to the front page.
core_workflows.purpose: This workflow demonstrates the ability to manage account settings multiple times and return to subreddit browsing.

screen_contexts.context: The screen displays the Reddit User Agreement for RedReader, a Reddit client. It states that RedReader accesses Reddit content via the Reddit API and prompts the user to read the terms and conditions and confirm acceptance.
The screen presents a settings menu offering options such as Appearance (with Font sub-option), Behavior, Images/Video, Cache, Network, Menus, Accessibility, Backup/Restore, and About.

The screen displays the RedReader application's front page with a time stamp at the top (11:30). It includes options such as 'All Subreddits', 'Find Location', 'Subscribed Subreddits', and a list of subreddit names such as 'art', 'dataisbeautiful', 'diy', 'documentaries', 'earthporn'. There is also a 'Log in' button and a note stating 'You are not currently logged in.' An option to 'Be anonymous' or 'Log in now' is also present.

This screen displays the RedReader application's front page, featuring options such as 'Find Location', 'Subscribed Subreddits', 'Reddit Accounts', and 'Add Reddit Account'. The active account is 'Anonymous'. Subscribed subreddits include 'creepy', 'dataisbeautiful', 'diy', and 'documentaries'.

The screen shows the RedReader application's front page with a list of pinned and subscribed subreddits. It includes a pinned subreddit '/t/diy' and a list of subscribed subreddits such as 'art', 'askreddit', 'askscience', 'aww', 'books', 'creepy', 'dataisbeautiful', and 'diy'.
This screen prompts the user to accept the Reddit user agreement to access content through the Reddit API. It provides options to view the agreement details.

This screen prompts the user to log in via a web browser. It includes a time stamp (11:30), the application name (RedReader), and instructions to log in by clicking 'Continue'. The screen also lists a few subscribed subreddits, such as 'creepy', 'dataisbeautiful', 'diy', and 'documentaries'. Additionally, there are options to 'Close' or 'Continue' the login process, and a help link. It also mentions 'Front Page', 'All Subreddits', and 'Find Location 4a'.

This screen displays a list of all subreddits within RedReader, with a time stamp (11:31) and the application name (RedReader) at the top. It includes the 'Subscribed Subreddits' section and a list of subreddit categories such as 'art', 'askreddit', 'askscience', 'aww', 'books', 'creepy', 'dataisbeautiful', 'diy', 'documentaries', and 'earthporn'. The 'Find Location' option mentioned is not present in the OCR text and has been removed.

This screen shows a list of pinned subreddits in RedReader with a time stamp (11:32) and the application name (RedReader) at the top. The screen includes options to 'Copy Link', 'View in External Browser', 'Share', and 'Block Subreddit'. It also provides an option to 'Unpin from Main Menu'. The listed subreddits include 'aww', 'books', 'creepy', 'dataisbeautiful', 'diy'.

The screen displays an error message indicating that the web page at https://www.reddit.com/api/v1/authorize.compact?response_type=code&duration=permanent&state=Te could not be loaded due to an internet connection issue (net::ERR_INTERNET_DISCONNECTED).

The screen indicates that the Reddit user agreement has not been accepted. It suggests that users must accept the agreement to access content through the Reddit API. The page provides options to view more details and to directly view the agreement.

The screen shows the front page of the RedReader app, displaying the top subreddit '/r/diy'. It also lists subscribed subreddits including 'art', 'askreddit', 'askscience', 'aww', 'books', and 'creepy'. Additionally, it mentions the option to 'Find Location' with the coordinates '<0,'.

The screen displays a network error message indicating that the web page at https://www.reddit.com/api/v1/authorize.compact?response_type=code&duration=permanent&state=Te could not be loaded due to an internet connection issue (net::ERR_INTERNET_DISCONNECTED).

The screen displays the front page of RedReader with options for 'Find Location', 'Pinned Subreddits', 'Share', 'Block Subreddit', 'Pin to Main Menu', and a list of subreddits including 'aww', 'books', 'creepy', 'dataisbeautiful', and 'diy'. There is also an option to 'View in External Browser'.

The screen shows the front page of RedReader with 'All Subreddits' and 'Pinned Subreddits' sections. It includes a list of subreddits such as 'art', 'askreddit', 'askscience', 'aww', 'books', 'creepy', 'dataisbeautiful', and 'diy'.

The screen indicates that the Reddit user agreement has not been accepted, and it prompts the user to accept it if they wish to access content through the Reddit API. It provides options to view details and the agreement itself.

The screen displays the front page of RedReader with 'All Subreddits' and 'Pinned Subreddits' sections. It includes a list of subreddits such as 'art', 'askreddit', 'askscience', 'aww', 'books', 'creepy', 'dataisbeautiful', and 'diy'.

The screen displays the Reddit front page with a timestamp '11:31'. It includes a header 'Front Page' and a URL 'reddit.com/hot'. Below the header, there is a message indicating that the Reddit user agreement has not been accepted, preventing access to content through the Reddit API. A button 'VIEW AGREEMENT DETAILS' is provided to allow users to review the agreement.

This screen displays the front page of a Reddit-like application with a timestamp at 11:31. It includes options to view the user agreement, navigate to past versions, submit posts, manage accounts, switch themes, and access settings. The screen also shows a call to action to accept the Reddit user agreement if one wishes to access certain content.

This screen allows the user to select a theme for the application. It provides options such as Night, Night (low contrast), Green, Blue, Light Blue, Orange, Gray, and Ultra Black. The screen also includes a copyright notice at the bottom, which reads 'OO0O0O0000000 ©'.

This screen provides options to adjust font scales for various elements in the application. The user can choose to use a global font scale for post titles, post subtitles, opened post titles and subtitles, comment/inbox headers, body text, and link buttons. The screen is timestamped at 11:33 243.

The screen appears to be a settings or configuration page. It includes options to adjust the global font scale with various size options ranging from 0.7x to 1.7x. There is a 'Cancel' button at the bottom, presumably to exit the settings without applying changes.

The screen displays font customization options for different elements in the application. It allows the user to select whether to use a global font scale or specify a custom scale for various sections such as post titles, subtitles, opened post titles, opened post subtitles, comment/inbox headers, body text, and link buttons. The comment/inbox headers section is set to use a custom scale of 0.7x, while the others are set to use the global font scale.

The screen displays subtitle settings options with a global font scale dropdown menu allowing users to select different font size options ranging from 0.7x to 1.7x. There is also a 'Cancel' button present.

This screen provides options for customizing font settings across different sections of the application. It includes options to set font scales for post titles, subtitles, opened post titles and subtitles, comment/inbox headers, body text, and link buttons. Each section specifies whether to use the global font scale or set a specific scale, except for post subtitles which are fixed at 1.1x and comment/inbox headers which are fixed at 0.7x.

The screen displays opened post titles and a global font scale adjustment feature. The available font scale options are 0.7x, 0.8x, 0.9x, Normal, 1.05x, 1.1x, 1.2x, 1.3x, 1.4x, 1.5x, and 1.6x. There is also a 'Cancel' button present on the screen.

This screen allows users to customize font settings for different elements of the application. It includes options for setting font scales for post titles, post subtitles, opened post titles, opened post subtitles, comment/inbox headers, body text, and link buttons. The customization options can either use the global font scale or set specific scales.

This screen provides a navigation menu for various settings categories. The categories include Appearance, Font, Behavior, Images/Video, Cache, Network, Menus, Accessibility, and Backup/Restore. The screen also includes an About section. Note: 'Behavior' in the context should be corrected to 'Behaviour' to match the OCR text.
